name: LxveOS
description: LxveAce security-panel OS (ESP-IDF): headless/GUI esp_console control surface — passive WiFi/BLE recon + defensive detectors, EAPOL/PMKID capture, an arm-gated offensive set, and the LXVEOS/1 serial event bridge. Per-board merged single .bin (flashed at 0x0). Authorized/defensive use.
note: Aligned to LxveAce/lxveos (PUBLIC). Tracks the rolling `ci-latest` GitHub PRERELEASE build channel: the github_release resolver hits the /releases/tags/ci-latest endpoint (not /releases/latest, which excludes prereleases). Each board ships one `<board_id>-merged.bin` (idf.py merge-bin embeds bootloader+partition-table+app), flashed at a single offset 0x0 — no separate bootloader/partition side-files. protocol=lxveos wires the LxveOSProtocol serial parser post-flash (LXVEOS/1 status/event framing). Boards: bare_esp32_headless / cyd_2432S028_classic / cyd_3248S035_r / m5stickc_plus2 (esp32), m5cardputer_v1 / jc3248w535_s3_qspi (esp32s3). The repo and the ci-latest channel are public, so each `<board>-merged.bin` downloads anonymously (no token). Flash write is owner-gated until HARDWARE-CLAIMS validation; bare_esp32_headless is HW-validated on ESP32-D0WD.
label: LxveOS (LxveAce)
